chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI H.C.A. No.34 of 2024 M/s Terry World Textile Versus Federation of Pakistan & others Date Order with signature of Judge 1. For orders on CMA 185/24 2. For orders on office objection a/w reply as at “A” 3. For orders on CMA 186/24 4. For hearing of main case. 5. For orders on CMA 187/24 Dated: 13.02.2024 M/s. Abdul Rahim Lakhani and Atta Muhammad Qureshi for appellant. -.-.- Against an order of adjudication a suit was filed as Suit No.1810 of 2023 before the original jurisdiction of this Court and injunction was obtained. Through the impugned order only the interim injunction order was recalled on the count that against the order of adjudication a forum, as identified in the order, is available. The only concern of the appellant is that an opportunity of hearing was not provided to the counsel as someone held his brief on the crucial date. We have given him liberty to argue if any forum is not available under the law, the only contention of the learned counsel is that there is a mala fide. Such consideration may also be taken into consideration if an appeal under the law, as identified in the order, may have been preferred. Be that as it may, we do not find any reason to interfere in the order as it has only recalled interim injunctive order passed earlier. However, at the conclusion of this order, learned counsel has requested that learned Single Judge may decide the fate of the suit at the earliest. Order accordingly. Insofar as discharge/release of security is concerned, learned counsel may move an application in this regard, which may be heard and decided by the learned Single Judge in accordance with law.

Order accordingly. Insofar as discharge/release of security is concerned, learned counsel may move an application in this regard, which may be heard and decided by the learned Single Judge in accordance with law. High Court Appeal stands disposed of in the above terms along with listed applications. Judge Judge
